recommendations.name: Margaret Chen
recommendations.explanation: Margaret has spent 18 years in elder care, with the last 8 focused specifically on dementia — and she holds a Certified Dementia Practitioner credential, so she genuinely understands what early-stage dementia looks like day to day, not just in theory. She describes herself as calm by nature, someone who never rushes the people she cares for, and she has a track record of working with families where privacy and personal space were a real priority. She is a non-smoker and a dog owner herself, so Biscuit will be in good hands. Her weekday availability from 7:00 AM to 1:00 PM lines up well with the mornings you need covered.

recommendations.name: Rosa Mendez
recommendations.explanation: Rosa has 22 years of experience caring for elderly people in their homes, including a decade as a primary caregiver for two separate dementia clients — this is someone who has seen what this kind of care really asks of a person and has chosen to keep showing up. She is calm and, as she puts it, does not believe in chatting all day — she believes in being present, which sounds very much in line with what matters to your family. She is a non-smoker, has cared for clients with dogs and cats, and holds both a Certified Dementia Practitioner and a Dementia Care Trainer certification. Her weekday mornings availability runs 7:30 AM to 12:30 PM.

recommendations.name: Donna Mitchell
recommendations.explanation: Donna brings 15 years of companion care experience and has spent 3 of those years working specifically with clients in early dementia — she understands that consistency and routine are not just helpful but essential for someone navigating memory changes. She describes herself as quiet and calm, someone who takes her cues from the person she is with and always respects a client's need for privacy and independence. She is a non-smoker and a dog owner, so she will be genuinely comfortable with Biscuit rather than just tolerant of him. She is available weekday mornings from 8:00 AM to 1:00 PM.

recommendations.name: James Okonkwo
recommendations.explanation: James brings exactly the kind of skilled, clinical background your father's recovery calls for — he spent 12 years as a Certified Nursing Assistant in a hospital rehabilitation unit before moving into private home care, and he holds certifications in both wound care support and medication management. He describes himself as someone with 'a clinical eye and a warm heart,' which speaks directly to what you're looking for: someone who is calm and steady, but also genuinely observant and detail-oriented enough to know when something needs attention. His skills cover every area you've outlined — safe mobility and transfers, post-surgical wound monitoring, medication timing, meal preparation, and physiotherapy exercise support. He is a non-smoker, and his availability on Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, and Friday aligns closely with your schedule, though you'll want to confirm Wednesday coverage with him directly.

recommendations.name: Patricia Williams
recommendations.explanation: Patricia has 14 years of experience caring for elderly individuals at home, including three years specifically supporting post-surgical recovery clients — so this isn't new territory for her. She is HHA-certified, CPR and First Aid trained, and her skills include medication reminders, bathing and personal hygiene assistance, and mobility support, which map well to the day-to-day care your father will need during recovery. Families who have worked with her describe her as warm and patient, and she emphasizes punctuality — both qualities that matter deeply when your father is depending on someone to show up and be present. Her schedule is a strong match: she's available Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, and Friday from 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM, and she is a non-smoker.

recommendations.name: Michael Torres
recommendations.explanation: Michael is a certified Home Health Aide with 7 years of experience, and his profile does list post-surgical recovery among his areas of advanced care support, along with mobility assistance and medication management. That said, we want to be straightforward with you: his prior experience is described in general terms through agency work, and his schedule is listed as flexible week-to-week rather than confirmed — which is worth clarifying directly before moving forward, given how important consistency is during your father's recovery. He is a non-smoker and comes in at a lower hourly rate, which may be a factor, but we'd encourage you to ask him specific questions about his hands-on post-surgical experience to make sure it matches what your father needs right now.

She describes her approach as being fully present rather than filling the room, which speaks directly to the unhurried, calm demeanor your family identified as essential. She is fluent in Spanish, CPR and First Aid certified, and holds a Home Health Aide certification, so the safety and communication pieces your family needs are genuinely covered. Entrusting someone with Eduardo's daily care is not a small thing, and Rosa is someone who has spent over two decades treating that trust as the core of her work.

She is CPR and First Aid certified, holds her Home Health Aide certification, and has hands-on experience supporting clients with mobility limitations, which is directly relevant to Eduardo's fall risk and need for safe movement support. Her schedule availability across four of your required days and her calm, relationship-centered approach make her a thoughtful option for your family to consider.

recommendations.name: Patricia Williams

She holds an advanced-level dementia care certification alongside her Certified Hospice and Palliative Nursing Assistant credential, and her skills include personal care and hygiene, medication reminders, and modified-texture meal preparation.
